section.title: My research path, from current work back to where I started.
section.intro: This timeline starts with my current work at Cornell and moves backward through the experiences that shaped how I think about biology, disease, and translation.

entries.title: Cornell University
entries.meta: Lab Technician III · Department of Biomedical Sciences
entries.description: I study breast cancer immunology, tumor plasticity, and response to checkpoint blockade, with a current focus on CD4+ T-cell function in CD73-deficient quasi-mesenchymal tumors.

entries.title: NYU Langone Health and NYU School of Global Public Health
entries.meta: Graduate Research Student and Graduate Research Assistant
entries.description: I worked on spatial immune organization in lung tumors and supported clinical study coordination, participant recruitment, and data collection in maternal health research.

entries.title: New York University
entries.description: My master's training strengthened my interest in translational biology and gave me research exposure across immunology, public health, and computational work.

entries.title: MNNIT Allahabad
entries.meta: Senior thesis in computational inhibitor discovery
entries.description: I used virtual screening, molecular docking, and pharmacokinetic analysis to identify potential inhibitors in Gram-negative bacterial secretion systems.

entries.title: IIT Guwahati
entries.description: I worked on the synthesis and characterization of methyl acrylamide cellulose nanowhiskers for environmental applications.

entries.title: Aurobindo Pharma
entries.description: I gained early exposure to translational biology, quality workflows, and how bench science connects to therapeutic production.

entries.title: MNNIT Allahabad
entries.meta: Early research in microbial fuel cells
entries.description: My first research project examined how environmental conditions influence microbial fuel-cell performance, which is where my interest in biological systems and context began.
